calls.top_strength: Rep quickly identified this as a >=1800 sqft home and smoothly pivoted to scheduling a virtual walkthrough on the spot rather than trying to force a phone estimate.
calls.coaching_note: This is an intake-to-walkthrough call, not a full pricing conversation, so it is not scored against the 85-pt rubric; no pricing, clock, or Save Your Ass content applies here.
calls.phone_presence_notes: Polite and functional ("take your time", "sounds good") but transactional, with mostly mechanical address/detail collection and little warmth beyond the basics.

calls.top_strength: Rep handled an upset customer (frustrated about a missed callback) gracefully, apologizing genuinely ("I am so sorry about that") and immediately looping in the RE rather than getting defensive.
calls.coaching_note: Call is a service-recovery + walkthrough-scheduling call, not a full pricing conversation, so it is not scored against the 85-pt rubric; no pricing/estimate/clock content applies.
calls.phone_presence_notes: Genuine apology and reassurance ("no worries at all", "I am so sorry") kept the customer calm and ended with a warm, appreciative close from both sides.

calls.top_strength: Rep proactively pulled up the daughter's existing booking profile and found the prior emailed estimate, saving the caller from re-explaining the whole move from scratch.
calls.coaching_note: No agenda statement, no hourly-rate/differentiator pitch (No Worries coverage, Meet Your Mover, On-Time Guarantee, no-hidden-fees), no clock explanation, and no booking attempt or email handoff — this reads like a quick estimate recap rather than a real sales conversation; walk through the full pricing script even on "just checking a number" calls.
calls.phone_presence_notes: Exchange is mostly transactional data-gathering with garbled/confused back-and-forth on numbers and no use of the caller's name.

calls.top_strength: Rep ran a genuinely thorough, patient room-by-room inventory with Mary ("let's just run through the list") and gave a clean, itemized pricing walkthrough — $192/hr, what's included, trip fee, 2.5-hr minimum, 15-min proration — that closed with an explicit "does that all make sense?" check.
calls.coaching_note: Pricing pitch skipped No Worries Damage Coverage, Meet Your Mover, On-Time Guarantee, and the full no-hidden-fees list, and there's no Save Your Ass prep speech at all — add these before the close, since this call has real efficiency-savings potential (30+ boxes, shed items) that never got mentioned.
calls.phone_presence_notes: Consistently warm and patient through a long, messy inventory list, closing with "my pleasure, have a great rest of your week" — good natural energy throughout.

calls.top_strength: Rep flexed the estimate in real time to match the customer's actual budget ("I really just wanted to get the big stuff"), restructuring to a 3-hour Thursday slot and dropping the price from $1,308 to $618 — strong budget-conscious problem-solving.
calls.coaching_note: Never stated the hourly rate explicitly, skipped the full differentiator list (No Worries coverage, Meet Your Mover, On-Time Guarantee, no-hidden-fees pitch) entirely, and no Save Your Ass prep tips were offered despite the customer's own request to minimize hours — an easy add given the customer was already trying to cut time.
calls.phone_presence_notes: Polite and accommodating with some warmth ("okeydokey", "have a great weekend") but largely task-focused amid repeated call-quality interruptions.

calls.top_strength: Rep gave a clean, complete no-hidden-fees pitch ("no travel fee, no credit card fees, no sterile fees, no disassemble/reassembly fees") and proactively surfaced a same-day-availability alternative (South Austin Tuesday slot) to lock the booking instead of losing it to a fully-booked schedule.
calls.coaching_note: Missed No Worries Damage Coverage, Meet Your Mover, and On-Time Guarantee entirely, and only touched the edge of the Save Your Ass concept ("more prepared you are, less time/money") without the consolidation examples or carload tip — add these to convert this already-strong call into a mastery-level one.
calls.phone_presence_notes: Patient and consistently warm through a confusing multi-stop move ("no worries", "sure, sure"), closing with a genuine "have a great weekend, bye guys."

calls.top_strength: Full differentiator pitch was delivered perfectly — hourly rate, inclusions, No Worries Damage Coverage, Meet Your Mover, On-Time Guarantee, and the complete no-hidden-fees list — and rep stayed calm and non-defensive when the customer disclosed distressing personal history and walked away.
calls.coaching_note: The estimate (6.5-7 hrs) landed well above the customer's anchor from a prior mover and no Save Your Ass prep speech was ever offered to show how she could shrink that time — leading with consolidation/carload tips before quoting hours might have prevented the walk-away here.
calls.phone_presence_notes: Calm and respectful under pressure but leaned on repeated scripted phrasing ("no problem ma'am, thank you so much" three times in a row) rather than a natural, individualized response to the customer's disclosure.

calls.top_strength: Textbook differentiator and clock delivery — hourly rate, full inclusions, trip fee disclosed transparently, No Worries coverage, Meet Your Mover, On-Time Guarantee, and no-hidden-fees pitch all covered clearly — and rep closed the loop by locking the booking with no deposit and a warm, personalized 4th of July sign-off.
calls.coaching_note: No Save Your Ass prep speech (consolidation, carload tip) was offered despite ~10-15 furniture items and 10+ boxes where prep could meaningfully cut time — add it before the close to round out an otherwise near-mastery call.
calls.phone_presence_notes: Warm and thorough throughout, with a genuine, personalized close ("thank you for being the best part of Einstein moving sir... happy 4th of July").

calls.top_strength: Joanna handled a complex freight-elevator/POD logistics puzzle patiently, confirming reservation windows, unit number, and an emergency contact for the leasing office.

calls.phone_presence_notes: Polite and helpful throughout ("that's good, that's perfect") but transactional, with little warmth beyond logistics confirmation.

calls.top_strength: Delivered a clean, complete differentiators pitch in one breath — No Worries Damage Coverage, Meet Your Mover, and On-Time Guarantee were all covered clearly ("if we run late... we provide discounts") along with the full no-hidden-fees list.
calls.coaching_note: Skipped the Save Your Ass prep speech entirely (0/10) and never mentioned no-deposit or office hours before ending the call — add these to close stronger and shorten the move.
calls.phone_presence_notes: Consistently polite ("sure, sure, of course") but leans on repeated filler phrases rather than varied, personalized warmth.

calls.phone_presence_notes: Used affirming language ("Absolutely!", "Sweet") and offered to run extra pricing scenarios unprompted, though the closing name slip was a rough note.

calls.top_strength: Handled the customer's anxiety about a past bad moving/insurance experience with genuine empathy ("I'm really sorry to hear about that") and walked through the damage claim process in detail.
calls.coaching_note: Never delivered the Save Your Ass prep speech or mentioned Meet Your Mover/On-Time Guarantee, and the call ran long (25.8 min) with meandering explanations — tighten the pacing.
calls.phone_presence_notes: Traded genuine humor with the customer (hoarding joke) and showed real empathy around his prior bad experience, with the customer staying engaged and appreciative throughout.

calls.top_strength: Clearly laid out the value-based day-of-week pricing with exact dollar comparisons ($218 vs $202/hr), giving the customer a concrete lever to save money.
calls.coaching_note: Never introduced No Worries Damage Coverage or the Save Your Ass prep speech, and left the close passive instead of directly asking to lock in the date.
calls.phone_presence_notes: Polite and helpful but fairly transactional, with a math mix-up on the rate difference that had to be self-corrected mid-call.

calls.top_strength: Defused the customer's apology for swearing with warmth and humor ("You're good, man"), keeping the call comfortable and leading straight into a same-call booking.
calls.coaching_note: Never mentioned the 2.5-hour minimum/clock mechanics, No Worries Damage Coverage, Meet Your Mover, On-Time Guarantee, or the Save Your Ass prep speech — build the full pitch into two-day pack-and-move bookings.
calls.phone_presence_notes: Handled an awkward moment (customer cursing then apologizing) with genuine humor and reassurance, and the customer booked on the spot.

calls.top_strength: Delivered one of the most complete clock and "what's included" breakdowns in the batch — explicit on-site clock start, 2.5-hour minimum, 15-minute proration, and a full no-hidden-fees rundown.
calls.coaching_note: Despite strong pricing mechanics, skipped No Worries Damage Coverage, Meet Your Mover, On-Time Guarantee, and the Save Your Ass prep speech entirely — those need to be standard on every full quote regardless of move size.
calls.phone_presence_notes: Professional, clear, and well-organized through a complex quote, but fairly transactional with little rapport-building language.

calls.top_strength: Patiently untangled the customer's confusion over the one-time vs. round-trip travel fee and right-sized the crew/truck count from a detailed room-by-room inventory (fridges, weights, fireplaces).
calls.coaching_note: No clock mechanics, damage coverage, Meet Your Mover, On-Time Guarantee, or Save Your Ass speech were covered, and the call ended on a scheduling conflict with no concrete next step proposed (e.g., booking through the north Austin branch).
calls.phone_presence_notes: Polite and clear despite call-quality dropouts, but transactional in tone with minimal warmth or affirmations.

calls.top_strength: Frank did strong inventory probing, catching non-obvious details like the piano-adjacent heavy art piece needing special wrap and the bed frame disassembly, and proactively flagged the loading dock truck-height issue that would affect timing.
calls.coaching_note: No agenda statement or pricing structure was given — jump straight from probing into a bottom-line number ('$1,894 for 8 hours') without walking through No Worries coverage, Meet Your Mover, On-Time Guarantee, or the no-hidden-fees pitch; build in the full differentiator script even for repeat customers.
calls.phone_presence_notes: Polite and familiar given the repeat-customer rapport ('we've done business before... you should be good'), but transitions are mostly transactional with few affirmations beyond 'okay' and 'yeah, alright.'

calls.top_strength: Frank patiently worked through a complex logistics coordination call (crew names, shirt sizes, gate security, dock door procedures) for an already-booked charity event, capturing every detail into notes for the branch manager.
calls.coaching_note: Not a sales call — no pricing, quote, or new booking discussion occurred; this is post-sale event logistics coordination for an existing corporate account, so the 85-point sales rubric doesn't apply.
calls.phone_presence_notes: Professional and responsive throughout, with a genuine warm moment thanking the customer for positive feedback about a prior crew ('Oh, absolutely! Yeah, I appreciate that'), but otherwise fairly procedural in tone.

calls.top_strength: Exceptional, detailed insurance explanation and budget-conscious estimating — Hector explicitly told the customer he builds estimates conservatively 'to avoid surprises in the budget' and walked through the $2,000 No Worries coverage in depth, which visibly reassured her.
calls.coaching_note: Never delivered the 'pricing is super simple' opener, Meet Your Mover email mention, On-Time Guarantee, or the full no-hidden-fees pitch (no CC fee/fuel surcharge/assembly fee) — these differentiators were skipped entirely despite a 39-minute call with ample time to include them.
calls.phone_presence_notes: Consistently warm and patient with phrases like 'no worries,' 'of course,' and 'absolutely,' and the customer ended the call unprompted praising him as 'very thorough.'

calls.top_strength: Hector clearly and specifically answered the customer's pre-move packing questions (padded blankets, shrink wrap, gondola boxes for art/mirrors, truck-loading order for fragile items), which directly resolved their concern.
calls.coaching_note: Not a sales call — customer already has a move booked for July 6th and called only to ask packing/logistics questions ahead of move day; no pricing or booking discussion took place, so the 85-point rubric doesn't apply.
calls.phone_presence_notes: Warm and reassuring throughout ('Sure thing,' 'Absolutely, no worries'), directly addressing the customer's underlying anxiety about their fragile items being damaged.
